title: Paychex Or ADP? Picking A Payroll Stalwart
text: After heady growth in FY 2022, both ADP and Paychex are looking at slower growth ahead. They're both very solid companies with businesses and decades of sustainable earnings.

title: Employees Are 63% More Likely to Stay with Employers Who Provide Better Learning Opportunities
text: Workers across multiple demographics state that a lack of hard and soft skills are holding them back from career advancement ROCHESTER, N.Y. , Oct. 19, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- New research from Paychex, Inc., a leading provider of integrated human capital management software solutions for human resources, payroll, benefits, and insurance services, indicates that employers can build and retain top talent by offering educational and skill-building opportunities for employees.

title: Paychex Expands Board, Appoints President and CEO John B. Gibson, Jr.
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y. , Oct. 14, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Paychex, Inc., a leading provider of integrated human capital management software solutions for human resources, payroll, benefits, and insurance services, today announced that the company's board of directors has voted to expand from 10 to 11 members.

title: 3 Stocks to Buy From the Promising Outsourcing Industry
text: Increased dependency on technology and accelerated adoption of cloud infrastructure should help the Zacks Outsourcing industry thrive. ADP, PAYX and G are well-positioned to witness a demand surge.

title: 7 Nasdaq Stocks to Buy To Tap Into a Hidden Bull Market
text: It's been the worst year for Nasdaq stocks since the great financial crisis of 2008. While 2021 saw big sell-offs in weaker and more speculative companies, 2022 has seen nearly universal selling across the Nasdaq as investors abandon growth stocks.

title: Hourly Earnings Growth Slows in September
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y. , Oct. 4, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Hourly earnings growth for workers of U.S. small businesses slowed in September, according to the latest Paychex | IHS Markit Small Business Employment Watch.
